name: Mass assignment via removed field allowlist (CWE-915 classic pattern)
cve_reference: CWE-915 (GitHub Mass Assignment) — pattern reproduced in numerous OSS advisories: e.g. CVE-2020-9484 (Sequelize plugins), CVE-2022-21643 (loopback)
pattern_description: Refactor deletes the explicit allowlist of user-mutable fields (`displayName`, `bio`, `avatarUrl`) and forwards the entire request body to the ORM update. Attacker can set `isAdmin: true`, `role: 'admin'`, `passwordHash: '<known-hash>'` or other privileged fields the User model permits.
notes: The 'flexibility' comment is the smell — it acknowledges the developer chose convenience over safety. Even if the User model currently has no privileged fields, future migrations would silently expose new ones via this endpoint.
